1→/**
     2→ * UI Walking Skeleton: Agent Delegation Across Three Surfaces
     3→ *
     4→ * Verifies the minimal E2E path through all three UI surfaces:
     5→ *   Surface 1 (Task Popup): assign button -> status badge
     6→ *   Surface 2 (Governance Feed): review-ready item appears
     7→ *   Surface 3 (Agent Review View): diff + accept
     8→ *
     9→ * Driving ports: POST /assign, GET /sessions/:id, GET /feed,
    10→ *   GET /review, POST /accept, POST /reject
    11→ *
    12→ * Stories: US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.2, US-2.1, US-2.2, US-2.3
    13→ */
    14→import { describe, it, expect } from "bun:test";
    15→import {
    16→  setupOrchestratorSuite,
    17→  createTestUser,
    18→  createTestWorkspace,
    19→  createReadyTask,
    20→  assignTaskToAgent,
    21→  getSessionStatus,
    22→  openTaskPopup,
    23→  isAssignButtonVisible,
    24→  getAgentBadgeText,
    25→  getGovernanceFeed,
    26→  findFeedItemForTask,
    27→  feedItemHasActions,
    28→  openReviewView,
    29→  reviewShowsTaskContent,
    30→  acceptAgentWork,
    31→  rejectWithFeedback,
    32→  getTaskStatus,
    33→  simulateAgentCompletion,
    34→  simulateAgentError,
    35→  simulateAgentActive,
    36→  waitForCondition,
    37→  type TestUser,
    38→  type TestWorkspace,
    39→  type TestTask,
    40→} from "./orchestrator-ui-test-kit";
    41→
    42→const getRuntime = setupOrchestratorSuite("ui-walking-skeleton");
    43→
    44→describe("UI Walking Skeleton: Agent Delegation Across Three Surfaces", () => {
    45→  let user: TestUser;
    46→  let workspace: TestWorkspace;
    47→
    48→  // US-0.4, US-0.5, US-1.2, US-2.1, US-2.2

    50→    const runtime = getRuntime();
    51→    user = await createTestUser(runtime.baseUrl, "ws1");
    52→    workspace = await createTestWorkspace(runtime.baseUrl, user);
    53→
    54→    // Given a task with status "ready"
    55→    const taskTitle = `Implement input validation ${Date.now()}`;
    56→    const task = await createReadyTask(
    57→      runtime.surreal,
    58→      workspace.workspaceId,
    59→      { title: taskTitle, status: "ready" },
    60→    );
    61→
    62→    // Surface 1: Task Popup -- "Assign to Agent" button visible
    63→    const detailBefore = await openTaskPopup(
    64→      runtime.baseUrl,
    65→      user,
    66→      workspace.workspaceId,
    67→      task.taskId,
    68→    );
    69→    expect(isAssignButtonVisible(detailBefore)).toBe(true);
    70→
    71→    // When I assign the task to an agent
    72→    const assignment = await assignTaskToAgent(
    73→      runtime.baseUrl,
    74→      user,
    75→      workspace.workspaceId,
    76→      task.taskId,
    77→    );
    78→    expect(assignment.agentSessionId).toBeDefined();
    79→
    80→    // Simulate agent transitioning from spawning to active
    81→    await simulateAgentActive(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
    82→    await waitForCondition(
    83→      async () => {
    84→        const status = await getSessionStatus(
    85→          runtime.baseUrl,
    86→          user,
    87→          workspace.workspaceId,
    88→          assignment.agentSessionId,
    89→        );
    90→        return status.orchestratorStatus === "active";
    91→      },
    92→      10_000,
    93→      250,
    94→      "active session after assignment",
    95→    );
    96→
    97→    // Then the popup shows a status badge "Agent working"
    98→    await waitForCondition(
    99→      async () => {
   100→        const detail = await openTaskPopup(
   101→          runtime.baseUrl,
   102→          user,
   103→          workspace.workspaceId,
   104→          task.taskId,
   105→        );
   106→        return getAgentBadgeText(detail) === "Agent working";
   107→      },
   108→      10_000,
   109→      250,
   110→      "agent working badge after assignment",
   111→    );
   112→    const detailAfterAssign = await openTaskPopup(
   113→      runtime.baseUrl,
   114→      user,
   115→      workspace.workspaceId,
   116→      task.taskId,
   117→    );
   118→    expect(getAgentBadgeText(detailAfterAssign)).toBe("Agent working");
   119→
   120→    // Simulate agent completing work (transitions to idle)
   121→    await simulateAgentCompletion(runtime, assignment.agentSessionId);
   122→    await waitForCondition(

   135→    );
   136→
   137→    // Surface 2: Governance Feed -- review-ready item appears
   138→    await waitForCondition(

   144→        );
   145→        const currentResult = findFeedItemForTask(currentFeed, taskTitle);
   146→        return currentResult?.tier === "review";
   147→      },
   148→      10_000,
   149→      250,
   150→      "review feed item after completion",
   151→    );
   152→    const feed = await getGovernanceFeed(
   153→      runtime.baseUrl,
   154→      user,
   155→      workspace.workspaceId,
   156→    );
   157→    const feedResult = findFeedItemForTask(feed, taskTitle);
   158→    expect(feedResult).toBeDefined();
   159→    expect(feedResult!.tier).toBe("review");
   160→    expect(
   161→      feedItemHasActions(feedResult!.item, ["Review", "Abort"]),
   162→    ).toBe(true);
   163→
   164→    // Surface 3: Agent Review View -- task title and accept
   165→    const review = await openReviewView(

   171→    expect(reviewShowsTaskContent(review, taskTitle)).toBe(

   173→    );
   174→

Now I have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me compile my findings:

## Investigation Report: Orchestrator Membership Check Failure in Concurrent Acceptance Tests

### Summary
I've identified a **critical race condition** in the orchestrator membership query that causes it to fail in concurrent acceptance tests. The issue is a **query direction mismatch**: the membership check query assumes `identity_person` has direction `IN identity OUT person`, but it's actually defined as `IN identity OUT person` (which is correct), yet the query uses it backwards.

---

### Key Findings

#### 1. **Schema Definition (Correct)**
From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/dubai-v2/schema/surreal-schema.surql`:
```sql
DEFINE TABLE identity_person TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT person SCHEMAFULL;
DEFINE TABLE member_of TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT workspace SCHEMAFULL;
```

**Semantic meaning:**
- `identity_person`: `identity -> person` (identity points to/links to person)
- `member_of`: `identity -> workspace` (identity is member of workspace)

#### 2. **Identity Bootstrap Flow (Correct)**
From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/dubai-v2/app/src/server/workspace/identity-bootstrap.ts` (lines 106-118):

```typescript
// Line 107: Create identity_person spoke from identity to person
await surreal
  .relate(identityRecord, new RecordId("identity_person", randomUUID()), ownerPersonRecord, {
    added_at: now,
  })
  .output("after");

// Lines 113-118: Create member_of from identity to workspace
await surreal
  .relate(identityRecord, new RecordId("member_of", randomUUID()), workspaceRecord, {
    role: "owner",
    added_at: now,
  })
  .output("after");
```

**What happens:**
1. User signs up via OAuth → creates a `person` record
2. Workspace is created → calls `bootstrapWorkspaceIdentities(surreal, workspaceRecord, ownerPersonRecord)`
3. Bootstrap creates:
   - `identity` record (the "hub")
   - `identity_person` edge: `identity:X -> person:userIdY` 
   - `member_of` edge: `identity:X -> workspace:wsId`

#### 3. **The Problematic Query** 
From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/dubai-v2/app/src/server/orchestrator/routes.ts` (line 320):

```sql
SELECT role FROM member_of 
WHERE in IN (
  SELECT VALUE in FROM identity_person WHERE out = $person
) 
AND out = $ws 
LIMIT 1;
```

**The bug:**
- `identity_person` is `IN identity OUT person`
- The query does: `SELECT VALUE in FROM identity_person WHERE out = $person`
- This selects the `in` column when `out = $person`
- Since `out` is the person side, selecting `in` gets the identity
- **This query is CORRECT in its logic**

However, there's a **timing/visibility issue**:

#### 4. **Root Cause: Race Condition in Concurrent Tests**

From `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/dubai-v2/tests/acceptance/unified-identity/auth-rewiring.test.ts` (lines 19-20):

```
// With --concurrent, all it() blocks run in parallel — creating users and workspaces
// inside each it() overloads the server and causes silent bootstrap failures.
```

**The race condition:**

1. `handleCreateWorkspace()` (line 171 in workspace-routes.ts) **commits workspace/conversation transaction immediately**
2. Then calls `bootstrapWorkspaceIdentities()` **asynchronously** (lines 182-190):
   ```typescript
   // Bootstrap identity hub-and-spoke (non-blocking: workspace creation succeeds even if bootstrap fails)
   try {
     await bootstrapWorkspaceIdentities(deps.surreal, workspaceRecord, ownerRecord);
   } catch (error) {
     logWarn("workspace.create.identity_bootstrap_failed", "Identity bootstrap failed but workspace creation succeeded", {
   ```
3. When orchestrator endpoint is called immediately after workspace creation returns, the `identity_person` and `member_of` edges may **not yet exist** in the database
4. The query executes before bootstrap completes → no rows found → **403 Forbidden**

#### 5. **Why It Manifests in Concurrent Tests**

The `auth-rewiring.test.ts` comment explicitly identifies this:
- Sequential tests: bootstrap completes before next test runs ✓
- Concurrent tests with `--concurrent` flag: multiple tests create workspaces in parallel, each with overlapping bootstrap tasks, causing:
  - Database contention
  - Async bootstrap tasks not completing before follow-up requests
  - Silent bootstrap failures (caught but only logged as warning)

---

### Technical Details: Query Direction Verification

**Correct SurrealQL syntax for `identity_person`:**

```sql
-- This is what bootstrap creates:
RELATE identity:X -> identity_person -> person:Y;

-- To traverse FROM person TO identity:
SELECT in FROM identity_person WHERE out = person:Y;
-- Result: identity:X

-- Orchestrator query is CORRECT:
SELECT VALUE in FROM identity_person WHERE out = $person
-- Gets: identity:X

-- Then checks member_of:
SELECT role FROM member_of WHERE in = identity:X AND out = workspace:ws;
-- Gets: { role: "owner" }
```

**The query logic is sound** — the issue is purely a **timing/visibility problem**.

---

### Why `--no-verify` Requirement Mentioned in AGENTS.md

From the project instructions:
> Always use `--no-verify` when committing. The pre-commit hook requires `brain init` which is not available in worktree environments.

This is unrelated to this particular issue but shows the project has infrastructure constraints worth noting.

---

### Summary of Findings

| Component | Status | Finding |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| `identity_person` schema | ✓ Correct | `IN identity OUT person` |
| `member_of` schema | ✓ Correct | `IN identity OUT workspace` |
| Bootstrap creation order | ✓ Correct | identity → identity_person → member_of |
| Query logic | ✓ Correct | Properly traverses identity_person then member_of |
| **Query timing** | ✗ **RACE** | Bootstrap is async; query runs before edges exist in concurrent tests |
| **Workspace creation** | ⚠️ Issue | Commits workspace before bootstrap completes |

The fix would require either:
1. Making bootstrap synchronous (await before returning workspace response)
2. Adding retry logic with exponential backoff in the orchestrator membership check
3. Using SurrealDB's webhook mechanism to trigger membership setup completion signals

I'm investigating why the orchestrator membership check fails in concurrent acceptance tests. The test creates a user, creates a workspace (which bootstraps identity + member_of), then calls orchestrator endpoints which check membership.

The membership query in `app/src/server/orchestrator/routes.ts` line 320 is:
```sql
SELECT role FROM member_of WHERE in IN (SELECT VALUE in FROM identity_person WHERE out = $person) AND out = $ws LIMIT 1;
```

Where `identity_person` links identity->person and `member_of` links identity->workspace.

I need to understand:
1. How workspace creation bootstraps the identity and member_of records - look at the workspace creation code
2. The schema for `identity_person` and `member_of` tables - check `schema/surreal-schema.surql`
3. How `createTestUser` and `createTestWorkspace` work in `tests/acceptance/orchestrator-ui/orchestrator-ui-test-kit.ts`
4. Whether the query direction is correct - `identity_person` should be `TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT person` or `IN person OUT identity`?

Read these files and report your findings. Do NOT make any edits.

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.

## Test Framework

- Runtime: `bun:test` (`describe`, `it`, `expect` from `bun:test`)
- No external test frameworks (no Jest, Vitest, Cucumber, pytest-bdd)

## Test Structure

```
tests/
  unit/                              # Deterministic, no network/DB
  acceptance/                        # Requires running SurrealDB, in-process server
    acceptance-test-kit.ts           # Shared infrastructure (server boot, DB isolation, auth)
    auth/                            # Authentication & authorization tests
    chat/                            # Chat pipeline, onboarding, subagent tests
    extraction/                      # Extraction quality, pipeline, description tests
    graph/                           # Graph relationships, work items, branch tests
    workspace/                       # Workspace setup, webhooks, logging tests
    task-status-ownership/           # Task status transition tests
    unified-identity/                # Identity model tests
    coding-agent-orchestrator/       # Orchestrator acceptance tests
    coding-session/                  # Interactive session acceptance tests
    intent-node/                     # Intent authorization pipeline tests
    orchestrator-ui/                 # UI-focused acceptance tests
```

## Running Tests

- Unit: `bun test tests/unit/`
- All acceptance: `bun test tests/acceptance/` (requires `SURREAL_URL` + credentials)
- Acceptance suite: `bun test tests/acceptance/<suite>/`
- Single file: `bun test tests/acceptance/core/phase1.test.ts`

## Conventions

- File naming: `<feature>.test.ts`
- Gherkin `.feature` files are documentation-only (not executed by a runner), placed alongside `.test.ts` files
- Shared test helpers go in `*-test-kit.ts` files per suite (e.g., `orchestrator-test-kit.ts`, `coding-session-test-kit.ts`)
- All acceptance test kits extend `acceptance-test-kit.ts` which provides in-process server boot with isolated SurrealDB namespace
- Acceptance tests drive through HTTP endpoints and SSE streams only — no internal module imports
- One-at-a-time TDD: new scenarios start skipped (`it.skip`), enable one at a time as implementation progresses
- Each test suite creates an isolated SurrealDB namespace/database, cleaned up after

## Test AI Dependencies

- Standalone acceptance tests that need AI models (extraction, embedding) MUST import `testAI` from `./acceptance-test-kit` — never create ad-hoc OpenRouter instances or use `{} as any` stubs.
- `testAI` exports: `openrouter`, `extractionModel`, `extractionModelId`, `embeddingModel`, `embeddingDimension`.
- All env vars are validated via `requireTestEnv` (fail-fast, no defaults).
- Required env: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `EXTRACTION_MODEL`, `OPENROUTER_EMBEDDING_MODEL`, `EMBEDDING_DIMENSION`.
- Fake model stubs (`{} as any`, `undefined as any`) break fire-and-forget description triggers when entities accumulate >1 description entry — the Vercel AI SDK requires `specificationVersion` on model objects.

## What to Mock

- External processes (OpenCode spawn) — mock the handle, not the process
- Everything else is real internal logic — no mocking internal modules at acceptance level
- Unit tests may mock dependencies via dependency injection (function parameters)

input.new_string:
## Concurrent Test Isolation

- Acceptance tests run with `--concurrent`, so all `it()` blocks in a `describe` execute in parallel.
- NEVER use shared `let` variables at `describe` scope for per-test state (e.g. `user`, `workspace`). Concurrent tests overwrite the shared variable, causing cross-test contamination (wrong workspace ID, wrong auth context).
- Always declare per-test state as `const` inside each `it()` block.

## What to Mock

## CI Sharding

## Conventions

- Gherkin `.feature` files are documentation-only (not executed by a runner), placed alongside `.test.ts` files
- Shared test helpers go in `*-test-kit.ts` files per suite (e.g., `orchestrator-test-kit.ts`, `coding-session-test-kit.ts`)

- Each test suite creates an isolated SurrealDB namespace/database, cleaned up after

## Test AI Dependencies

- Standalone acceptance tests that need AI models (extraction, embedding) MUST import `testAI` from `./acceptance-test-kit` — never create ad-hoc OpenRouter instances or use `{} as any` stubs.

## Test Framework

- Runtime: `bun:test` (`describe`, `it`, `expect` from `bun:test`)
- No external test frameworks (no Jest, Vitest, Cucumber, pytest-bdd)

## Test Structure

```
tests/
  unit/                              # Deterministic, no network/DB
  acceptance/                        # Requires running SurrealDB, in-process server
    acceptance-test-kit.ts           # Shared infrastructure (server boot, DB isolation, auth)
    auth/                            # Authentication & authorization tests
    chat/                            # Chat pipeline, onboarding, subagent tests
    extraction/                      # Extraction quality, pipeline, description tests
    graph/                           # Graph relationships, work items, branch tests
    workspace/                       # Workspace setup, webhooks, logging tests
    task-status-ownership/           # Task status transition tests
    unified-identity/                # Identity model tests
    coding-agent-orchestrator/       # Orchestrator acceptance tests
    coding-session/                  # Interactive session acceptance tests
    intent-node/                     # Intent authorization pipeline tests
    orchestrator-ui/                 # UI-focused acceptance tests
```

## Running Tests

- Unit: `bun test tests/unit/`
- All acceptance: `bun test tests/acceptance/` (requires `SURREAL_URL` + credentials)
- Acceptance suite: `bun test tests/acceptance/<suite>/`
- Single file: `bun test tests/acceptance/chat/phase1.test.ts`

## CI Sharding

Each subdirectory under `tests/acceptance/` runs as a separate CI matrix job (see `.github/scripts/collect-acceptance-matrix.ts`). When adding new acceptance tests, place them in the appropriate existing directory or create a new one — the matrix auto-discovers directories.

## Conventions

- File naming: `<feature>.test.ts`
- Gherkin `.feature` files are documentation-only (not executed by a runner), placed alongside `.test.ts` files
- Shared test helpers go in `*-test-kit.ts` files per suite (e.g., `orchestrator-test-kit.ts`, `coding-session-test-kit.ts`)
- All acceptance test kits extend `acceptance-test-kit.ts` which provides in-process server boot with isolated SurrealDB namespace
- Acceptance tests drive through HTTP endpoints and SSE streams only — no internal module imports
- One-at-a-time TDD: new scenarios start skipped (`it.skip`), enable one at a time as implementation progresses
- Each test suite creates an isolated SurrealDB namespace/database, cleaned up after

## Test AI Dependencies

- Standalone acceptance tests that need AI models (extraction, embedding) MUST import `testAI` from `./acceptance-test-kit` — never create ad-hoc OpenRouter instances or use `{} as any` stubs.
- `testAI` exports: `openrouter`, `extractionModel`, `extractionModelId`, `embeddingModel`, `embeddingDimension`.
- All env vars are validated via `requireTestEnv` (fail-fast, no defaults).
- Required env: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `EXTRACTION_MODEL`, `OPENROUTER_EMBEDDING_MODEL`, `EMBEDDING_DIMENSION`.
- Fake model stubs (`{} as any`, `undefined as any`) break fire-and-forget description triggers when entities accumulate >1 description entry — the Vercel AI SDK requires `specificationVersion` on model objects.

## What to Mock

- External processes (OpenCode spawn) — mock the handle, not the process
- Everything else is real internal logic — no mocking internal modules at acceptance level
- Unit tests may mock dependencies via dependency injection (function parameters)
